title: Memory Management and Memory Safety in Modern C23

audience.description: Learners who can reason about data structures and algorithms but are new to C and need to design, test, diagnose, optimize, and defend explicit memory and resource lifetimes from first principles through production systems.
entryKnowledge: Trace common data structures and algorithms, state invariants, analyze asymptotic cost, and test changed and adversarial inputs.
Use a terminal, edit source files, interpret test failures, and explain rather than merely report program output.
deviceConstraints: Modern browser. Instant code practice runs only in a disposable Web Worker using PicoC 3.2.2, a clearly labeled C89/C90 and selected C99 subset rebuilt with Emscripten 6.0.3. C23 features, GCC and Clang diagnostics, native ABI, threads, sanitizers, fuzzing, profiling, and operating-system resources require explicit controlled transfer gates.
accessibilityAssumptions: Memory maps, pointer traces, allocation timelines, object graphs, sanitizer output, tables, code, terminals, charts, and status use structured text alternatives, keyboard operation, visible focus, announced updates, large targets, reduced motion, and no color-only meaning.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Trace common data structures and algorithms, state invariants, analyze asymptotic cost, and test changed and adversarial inputs.
A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Use a terminal, edit source files, interpret test failures, and explain rather than merely report program output.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 5 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: C23 foundations needed for memory work; translation, types, conversions, objects, lifetime, representation, alignment, arrays, pointers, strings, structures, unions, callbacks, automatic storage, heap allocation, size arithmetic, ownership, cleanup, realloc, temporal and spatial defects, initialization, undefined behavior, aliasing, provenance, arenas, pools, reference counting, tracing collection, external resources, concurrency, ABI, FFI, WebAssembly linear memory, sanitizers, static analysis, fuzzing, profiling, hardening, testing, release, rollback, and incident defense

More than two hundred exact runnable changed-case C programs in isolated browser practice, plus explicit C23, GCC 16, Clang 22, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, thread, Python, Rust, WebAssembly, load, fault, release, and production transfer evidence
Five cumulative stakeholder projects and a performance-based memory-safety certification defense
excludes: Claiming that PicoC implements C23 or proves native compiler optimization, ABI, sanitizer, concurrency, operating-system, foreign-runtime, load, or production behavior
Sending learner source to a host process, server compiler, network, filesystem, shell, or production system; unsafe legacy APIs appear only in bounded defect diagnosis and migration work

sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves earlier size, bounds, lifetime, ownership, cleanup, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding one bounded memory concept.
Every coding task produces changed-case output and an inspectable invariant. Code shape alone, a clean compile, one passing browser run, or one sanitizer run is never sufficient mastery evidence.

Browser code stays inside the PicoC subset and disposable worker. C23 syntax and semantics, optimizing compilers, native libraries, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production claims name an explicit controlled transfer gate.
Unsafe examples are presented as defects to predict, diagnose, repair, and regression-test; they are never copied into starter patterns as acceptable production code.

competencies.statement: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.
misconceptions: A browser interpreter proves current native compiler, sanitizer, ABI, and operating-system behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.

competencies.statement: Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
misconceptions: Selecting a C23 mode makes every C23 facility available and identical on every compiler and library.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.

competencies.statement: Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
misconceptions: If a program starts, every earlier translation and linkage assumption was correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.

competencies.statement: Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.

competencies.statement: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compile with strict language mode, high-value warnings, warnings as errors, debug information, and a documented extension policy.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose duplicate, missing, hidden, weak, and mismatched symbols from object and linker evidence.

competencies.statement: Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
misconceptions: Setting one local pointer to null prevents other aliases from freeing the block.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.

competencies.statement: Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
misconceptions: An interior pointer remains usable for its offset after the base is freed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.

competencies.statement: Use nulling as a local state aid without claiming it repairs aliases, temporal races, or ownership ambiguity.
misconceptions: Free automatically changes every copy of the pointer to null.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use nulling as a local state aid without claiming it repairs aliases, temporal races, or ownership ambiguity.

competencies.statement: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.
misconceptions: Address uniqueness across time is a dependable object identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.

competencies.statement: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
misconceptions: Testing length one and a typical length catches every off-by-one defect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.

competencies.statement: Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
misconceptions: The destination size alone determines whether a copy is safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.

competencies.statement: Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.
misconceptions: memcpy implementations always behave like memmove on small overlaps.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.

competencies.statement: Prevent subtraction and backward scans from forming pointers before the first element.
misconceptions: A pointer may temporarily move before an array if it is not dereferenced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent subtraction and backward scans from forming pointers before the first element.

competencies.statement: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.
misconceptions: A fortified build makes all dynamic buffer operations memory safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.

competencies.statement: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
misconceptions: malloc and automatic storage begin as zero-filled objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.

competencies.statement: Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
misconceptions: Reading any uninitialized unsigned integer merely gives an unpredictable ordinary value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.

competencies.statement: Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
misconceptions: Undefined behavior must produce a crash or diagnostic at the offending statement.

misconceptions: All portable variability is undefined behavior.

competencies.statement: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.

competencies.statement: Choose size classes, alignment, page ownership, and internal-fragmentation metrics from workload evidence.
misconceptions: One size class minimizes both waste and allocator overhead.

competencies.statement: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.
misconceptions: RSS alone identifies allocator fragmentation.

competencies.statement: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
misconceptions: Every pointer copy should increment a reference count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.

misconceptions: Reference counts cannot overflow in realistic programs.

misconceptions: A weak pointer can be safely inspected after the object is destroyed.

competencies.statement: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.

competencies.statement: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
misconceptions: An unreachable object is necessarily unused and safe to finalize immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.

misconceptions: A single graph walk is enough without a complete allocation registry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.

competencies.statement: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.
misconceptions: Conservative collection finds every true pointer and no false pointer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.

competencies.statement: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.
misconceptions: A garbage collector provides deterministic file and lock release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.

misconceptions: Concurrent collection removes every stop-the-world phase and synchronization cost.

competencies.statement: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
misconceptions: Memory leak tools detect every leaked file, descriptor, socket, and lock.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.

competencies.statement: Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
misconceptions: Closing or destroying a resource twice is harmless if free(NULL) is harmless.

competencies.statement: Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
misconceptions: Compiler cleanup attributes have standard C23 semantics on every toolchain.

competencies.statement: Record which thread owns each lock and guarantee unlock on normal, error, cancellation, and early-return paths.
misconceptions: Freeing an object containing a locked mutex releases the lock safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record which thread owns each lock and guarantee unlock on normal, error, cancellation, and early-return paths.

competencies.statement: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.
misconceptions: A structure assignment safely duplicates ownership of every nested handle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

competencies.statement: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
misconceptions: Aligned word reads and writes are automatically race-free.

competencies.statement: Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
misconceptions: Atomic variables make neighboring non-atomic data visible in every order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.

competencies.statement: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
misconceptions: A release store alone prevents an object from being freed too soon.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.

competencies.statement: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.
misconceptions: Lock-free deletion may free a node immediately after unlinking it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.

competencies.statement: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.
misconceptions: A clean ThreadSanitizer run proves all schedules race-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

competencies.statement: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
misconceptions: A shared C header guarantees binary compatibility across all targets.

competencies.statement: Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.

misconceptions: A pointer and null terminator fully describe every foreign buffer.

misconceptions: A WebAssembly pointer is a host pointer and remains valid after memory growth.

misconceptions: Running every tool once is better than selecting compatible complementary gates.

competencies.statement: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
misconceptions: The number of malloc calls alone explains memory performance.

competencies.statement: Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
misconceptions: Smaller source code or fewer pointers guarantees better cache locality.

misconceptions: A benchmark winner on one micro-test is the best allocator for production.

competencies.statement: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.

competencies.statement: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.
misconceptions: A faster benchmark permits weaker cleanup or failure behavior.
